findings.message: Layering violation: route handler directly manipulates session state (session.commit, session.refresh). Transaction management should be encapsulated in the data-access layer.
findings.suggestion: Move session.commit() and session.refresh() into item_service.add_item() so the route handler receives a fully-persisted object. The data-access layer should own transaction boundaries.

findings.message: Duplicate data-access layers: item_service.py and repository.py both provide overlapping functions (add_item/create_item, fetch_all_items/list_items). This duplication creates maintenance burden and inconsistent APIs.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate into a single data-access layer. Choose one module (repository.py or item_service.py) and remove the duplicate. Ensure consistent transaction handling across both functions.
